description: Dynamic bridge — 2026-05-15 session — Relief rally Day 2, Nifty +1.18% to 23,689.60, Brent cooled to $105.49, BankNifty reclaimed 54,000+

market_regime.label: RELIEF_RALLY_DAY2_BRENT_COOLING
market_regime.key_risk: Nifty surged +1.18% on Brent cooling ($108.10→$105.49) and Trump-Xi summit optimism. BankNifty reclaimed 54,000+ (+1.26%). Broad-based buying. Relief rally Day 1 was strong — Day 2 follow-through is the key question. Brent below $106 is the floor for continued rally.
market_regime.vix_level: Elevated but declining on relief rally
market_regime.fii_stance: Likely net sellers but reduced intensity given crude cooling
market_regime.dii_stance: Continued buying — DII flows provided floor during selloff and now participate in rally

key_sectors_today.note: Broad-based rally but IT continued to lag. Banking recovery key signal.

key_drivers: Nifty snapped 4-day losing streak with positive breadth — overnight sentiment should carry mild positive bias.
Brent at $108.10, stable (+0.31%) — no fresh crude shock overnight expected unless Hormuz escalation.
Gold import duty hike digestion — Day 2 effects may see some normalization in commodity ETFs after +5-8% surges.
US markets: Trump-Xi meeting wildcard — any positive signal could amplify gap-up; negative signal caps at flat.
BankNifty at 53,456 still below 54,000 — banking needs catalyst for meaningful recovery; if global cues positive, short-covering could push gap-up to +0.3%.
risks: UPSIDE: Trump-Xi trade/diplomacy positive signal or Iran diplomatic breakthrough overnight → gap-up +0.3-0.5%.
DOWNSIDE: Brent breaks $110 overnight or fresh Hormuz incident → gap-down -0.3% to -0.5%.
DATA: India WPI or any scheduled macro print could shift open.

session_direction.key_assumption: Day 4 of re-escalation regime but Day 2 of recovery attempt. The 4-day selling dumped Nifty -3.3% (May 8-12); today's +0.14% is a tentative floor. If Brent holds $106-110 (no breakout), Nifty should consolidate 23,300-23,600. BankNifty must reclaim 54,000 for broader index to push higher. DII buying provides floor at 23,300. Upside capped at 23,600 (May 12 open area) without a geopolitical catalyst. Probability-weighted toward the Choppy Flat scenario as neither bulls nor bears have a clear catalyst.
session_direction.brent_forecast: $106-110 range. Stable to slight upward drift. Tail risk $112+ on Hormuz.

scenarios.name: RANGE_BOUND_POSITIVE (40%)
scenarios.description: Brent stable $106-109. Nifty drifts up toward 23,500-23,600 on continued sectoral rotation. DII supports. Close +0.2-0.5%.

scenarios.name: CHOPPY_FLAT (30%)
scenarios.description: No catalyst either way. Nifty oscillates 23,350-23,500. BankNifty stays weak. Close near flat to +0.1%.

scenarios.name: BEARISH_REVERSAL (20%)
scenarios.description: Brent breaks $110 or fresh FII selling wave. Today's bounce was a dead cat. Nifty retests 23,300-23,200. Close -0.3-0.7%.

scenarios.name: RELIEF_RALLY (10%)
scenarios.description: Trump-Xi positive / Iran diplomatic surprise. Nifty surges to 23,600-23,800. BankNifty reclaims 54,000+. Close +0.5-1.0%.

ceasefire_binary_matrix.note: Trump REJECTED Iran proposal — peace_deal_advancing collapsed from 0.35 → 0.10. Escalation upgraded from 0.30 → 0.60 on IRGC frigate incident + Brent spike to $115 + Trump explicit rejection. Stalemate at 0.30 (Trump did pivot to "very positive discussions" by May 4 — diplomacy not fully dead).

opening_bias_actual.scoring_note: Forecast was GAP_DOWN_SMALL (-0.25%, GIFT ~23,320). Actual was essentially flat / slight gap-up: Nifty opened at 23,382.85 (+3.30 pts, +0.014%) vs prior close 23,379.55. GIFT Nifty pre-open at 23,476 (+51 pts) was 156 points HIGHER than estimate of 23,320. Direction is WRONG — forecast called for continuation of the prior-day selloff (-1.83%), market instead opened flat, suggesting the oversold bounce mechanic asserted itself overnight despite Brent still at $107 and rupee near 96. Magnitude tiny in absolute terms (both near zero), but direction sign is opposite. Lesson: re-escalation Day 3 + record-oversold prints often produce flat/up gaps as systematic short-covering precedes session.
